content:
- Finished the task implementation: added `docs/tls-configuration.md`, new TLS docs contract tests/support, and cleaned README so the dedicated doc owns the TLS comparison boundary.
- Required lanes all passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; did not run `make test-long` because this is not a story-end task.
- Marked task 10 complete with `<passes>true</passes>`, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully, and next is to stage everything (including `.ralph` artifacts), commit with the validation evidence, push, and stop.

- Read previous progress and Ralph task summaries.
- Found `.ralph/current_tasks.md` contains only the generated header with no active tasks listed; `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` contains the completed task history.
- Next: follow the empty-active-task branch by running `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`; if any lane fails, create bug tasks before selecting anything.

- Confirmed actual repo stack before validation: Rust workspace (`Cargo.toml`, multiple crates under `crates/`) plus a Go subtree at `cockroachdb_molt/molt`.
- `Makefile` currently defines `check` and `lint` as `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `test` runs `cargo test --workspace` and `go test ./cmd/verifyservice` in the Go subtree.
- Next: run `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` exactly as required; if any lane fails, create bug tasks instead of selecting a completed task.

- Ran the empty-backlog validation branch because `.ralph/current_tasks.md` had no active tasks.
- Validation passed cleanly: `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all exited 0. The repo is a Rust workspace with a Go verifyservice subtree, and both stacks passed their required gates.
- Next after quitting: none for task selection. Touched `.ralph/STOP` because there are no active tasks and all required validations are green.

- Read Ralph progress, active task summary, done-task ledger, and representative repo files (`Cargo.toml`, `Makefile`, `cockroachdb_molt/molt/go.mod`) to confirm the actual stack: Rust workspace for the main toolchain plus a Go verifyservice subtree.
- Evaluated all active candidates in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`: one failing bug with `<priority>high</priority>` and three failing story-28 tasks; no `<blocked_by>` tags were present on the active candidates.
- Applied the mandatory selection precedence and bug-first rule, then wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` to the unblocked high-priority bug task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests.md`.
- Next after quitting immediately due to context limit: execute that bug task, remove brittle file-structure/text-scanning tests, replace only genuinely needed coverage with behavioral tests, and validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all pass.

- Read previous Ralph progress, active task pointer, the selected bug task, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed this task has no existing execution plan marker, so this turn must produce a plan file ending in `TO BE VERIFIED` and then stop.
- Audited representative brittle-test surfaces: `crates/runner/tests/support/runner_docker_contract.rs`, `crates/runner/tests/support/verify_source_contract.rs`, and `crates/runner/tests/support/tls_reference_surface.rs`; the recurring smell is wrong-place test logic that reads adjacent source/docs/Dockerfiles instead of exercising product behavior.
- Next: finish locating the concrete test modules to mention in the plan, write the bug plan under `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests_plans/`, and stop immediately.

- Created the planning artifact for the active high-priority bug at `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests_plans/2026-04-27-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests-plan.md`.
- The plan explicitly uses `tdd` only for real behavioral replacement coverage and uses `improve-code-boundaries` to delete wrong-place support modules that scan README/docs/Dockerfiles/compose/source trees instead of exercising runtime behavior.
- Identified the main dumb-test families to remove or reduce during execution: README/TLS doc scanners, source-tree and helper-name scanners, Dockerfile/cache-layer text scanners, and compose YAML shape scanners.
- Next after quitting immediately due to workflow: review the plan, flip it to `NOW EXECUTE` once the design is accepted, then execute the deletions/refactors and finish with `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the active Ralph task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests.md`.
- Re-read the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions for this turn.
- Next I am checking the task file and the existing plan artifact to decide whether this turn is planning-only or plan execution.

- Read the active bug task and the existing plan file; the plan currently ends in `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- I am validating the plan against the repository by scanning the tracked tests for adjacent-file readers and text-structure assertions.
- If the plan matches the actual dumb-test surfaces cleanly, the next step is to flip the plan to `NOW EXECUTE` and stop immediately per Ralph workflow.

- Verified the existing plan against concrete test files including README/TLS scanners, source-structure scanners, and E2E helper-name/source-marker scanners.
- The plan is sound: these tests are clearly asserting repo text, helper placement, or adjacent file contents rather than runtime behavior.
- Next I am flipping the plan terminator from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE` and then stopping immediately so the following turn can execute the plan.

- Confirmed by direct inspection that the targeted tests are dumb structure/content tests: they assert README headings/snippets, TLS doc wording, source-tree/helper markers, and adjacent file contents.
- Updated the existing plan file `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests_plans/2026-04-27-remove-dumb-structure-and-file-content-tests-plan.md` to end with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next after quitting immediately due to workflow: read the `NOW EXECUTE` plan, run the red-green cleanup slices, remove the dumb tests, replace only real behavioral gaps with honest tests, and finish with `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Read Ralph startup state, confirmed active task path, and re-read the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed prior progress says the existing plan was already validated and flipped to `NOW EXECUTE`; this turn should execute, not redesign.
- Next I am reading the bug task file and its plan, then removing the brittle structure/content tests in red-green slices and validating the repo with `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Deleted the pure README/TLS/source-tree/help-surface scanner tests and their dead support files.
- Reduced compose and image contracts to behavior that goes through real Docker/Compose/runtime boundaries, and removed Dockerfile/cache-layer string-pin tests.
- Next I am running the required validation lanes, fixing any compile/lint/test fallout, and then I will update the task metadata only if all required checks are green.

- Fixed the one stale setup-sql harness import exposed by `make check`; `make check` now passes cleanly.
- No design reversal was needed: the deletions compile after trimming dead helper APIs.
- Next I am running `make lint` and `make test`; if they stay green, I will update the task file, switch task, commit, and push.

- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after removing the brittle scanner suites and trimming dead helper code.
- The remaining contracts are behavioral: Docker image build/runtime checks, Compose/runtime checks, HTTP checks, DB checks, and log/event shape checks through public boundaries.
- Next I am updating the bug task metadata to passing, running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then staging, committing, and pushing all changes including `.ralph` artifacts.
